page: Amazon Web Services m8id.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores with hyper-threading running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a massive 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing low-latency local storage directly attached to the host. Network performance is defined by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark testing, the m8id.4xlarge demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, achieving a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2204.0 and a top-tier text processing score of 2984.0. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also show strong performance, with gzip compression reaching 34.13 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 instruction sets support efficient execution, as evidenced by a top-tier text generation speed of 350.53 tokens/sec for a small 135M parameter LLM. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range, with a Passmark CPU score of 29279.13 and a Redis SET performance of 30.29 million operations per second.

The inclusion of high-speed local NVMe storage alongside DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon cores makes the m8id.4xlarge a resource-dense option for workloads requiring fast local scratch space. It is well-suited for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale machine learning inference. The balanced resource allocation provides a reliable baseline for general-purpose enterprise applications without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8id.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by top-tier Geekbench single-core and text processing scores, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression. The CPU features Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, which facilitate efficient local LLM text generation. With an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the m8id.4xlarge provides a balanced, resource-dense profile. It is highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from fast local instance storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and text-processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.4xlarge features 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and 950 GB NVMe SSD storage for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing, high-speed DDR5 memory, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs and 480 MB L3 cache
64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
950 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Suited for databases, web serving, and software compilation
